data.title: Sleep Number holder Stadium Capital nominates four for board (update)
data.description:
Update 3:15pm: Adds Sleep Number response.Sleep Number's (NASDAQ:SNBR) largest shareholder Stadium Capital nominated four directors amid a proxy fight with the mattress retailer.

Stadium Capital, which is Sleep Number's (SNBR) largest holder with an 11.7% stake, nominated Patrick A. Hopf, Jeffrey T. Jackson, Jessica M. Prager and Kevin Baker to the company's board, according to a letter sent to SBNR's board on Monday.

The letter comes after Stadium a week ago threatened a new proxy fight with the mattress retailer. Stadium said it wanted an executive chairman appointed to run the company as CEO Shelly Ibach exits the company, which Sleep Number (SNBR) announced late last month.

"We did not come to this decision lightly, as we strongly prefer private engagement with the management teams and boards of our portfolio companies on ideas for value creation and improved governance," Stadium Capital wrote in the letter.

Stadium last September threatened a proxy fight and then came to an agreement with Sleep Number (SNBR) to add two directors to the board.

"We strongly believe it is in shareholders’ best interest to support the Board at this critical time," Sleep Number said in a statement emailed to Seeking Alpha on Monday. "There is no pretext for Stadium’s disruptive action, and we are disappointed that Stadium has taken this approach. Stadium has not provided any CEO candidates or input on the profile. "

"Nevertheless, the Board will carefully review Stadium’s director candidates and expects to interview them," Sleep Number continued in its response. "In the meantime, the Board remains focused on identifying and recruiting the optimal CEO and overseeing the Company’s continued business transformation."

Shares of Sleep Number (SNBR) rose 1.7% on Monday.

data.title: Top quant-rated natural gas stocks: ATO, NJR, SPH
data.description:
Inflows into natural gas (NYSEARCA:UNG) continued to grow last week, up 7.5%, according to BofA Securities.

Flows to the commodity (UNG), (BOIL), (UNL) are up 24.2% month-to-date, and up 33.8% year-to-date.

data.title: Merus rises 10% following updated phase 2 data on petosemtamab for head and neck cancer
data.description:
• Merus (NASDAQ:MRUS) is up ~10% in Monday trading after releasing updated data from a phase 2 trial on petosemtamab as a second-line+ treatment for those with head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.
  • As of a March 6, 2024, cutoff, 42 patients were randomized to the 1500 mg and 1100 mg doses. At 1500 mg, 12 were evaluable for response. Five esponses were observed including 1 complete response, 3 partial responses, and 1 unconfirmed partial response that was confirmed post cutoff.
  • At 1100 mg, 10 patients were evaluable with one confirmed partial response observed.
  • Also, no grade 5 treatment emergent adverse events were reported.
  • Separately, Merus also announced it signed an agreement with privately held Partner Therapeutics, giving the latter an exclusive right to commercialize zenocutuzumab, currently in phase 1/2, for the treatment of NRG1 fusion-positive (NRG1+) cancer in the U.S.

data.title: Apple faces employee lawsuit under updated California act: report
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) faces a civil suit filed by a former employee accusing the company of silencing employees and spying on its workers.

The suit, filed Sunday in California state court by Amar Bhakta, who currently works in digital advertising for Apple, alleges employees are prohibited from discussing working conditions. It goes further, alleging employees are required to allow the tech giant to "engage in physical, video and electronic surveillance," even when they are home and off work, according to a report by Semafor.

"Apple's surveillance policies and practices chill, and thus also unlawfully restrain, employee whistleblowing, competition, freedom of employee movement in the job market, and freedom of speech," the lawsuit said, according to a report by Reuters.

Bhakta is represented by Chris Baker of Baker Dolinko & Schwartz, and Jahan Sagafi, of Outten & Golden. According to Bhakta's LinkedIn page, he was an ad operations manager at Hulu for three years before joining Apple in 2020.

The lawsuit was filed under a newly updated version of California's Private Attorneys General Act, or PAGA. PAGA makes it easier for employees to sue employers on behalf of the state. First established in 2004, it was updated this year by designating 65% of any penalties won in a lawsuit to go to the California Labor and Workforce Development Agency, while 35% goes to aggrieved employees. Previously, 75% went to the state and 25% to employees.

Apple is well known for its strong belief in privacy. Its delayed entrance into the artificial intelligence race was due in part to developing Apple Intelligence, which could be powered on-device rather than in the cloud. The iPhone is also regularly labeled as the most difficult smartphone to hack.

data.title: Steel stocks rise as Goldman takes positive view; likes Cliffs, Commercial Metals, Nucor
data.description:
Cleveland-Cliffs (NYSE:CLF) +3.2%, Commercial Metals (CMC) +2.5% and Nucor (NUE) +1% as Goldman Sachs initiates coverage with Buy ratings with a constructive view of the broader U.S. steel industry.

Goldman's Mike Harris says the prevailing sentiment towards the U.S. steel industry seems too pessimistic given concerns on global oversupply and weak but improving pricing; he is more positive, believing that "both cyclical (steady demand and lower interest rates) and structural (fiscal stimulus and favorable trade policy) factors could drive earnings growth for the domestic steel industry despite a weaker global backdrop."

The analyst sees Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) and Commercial Metals (CMC) as ways to "leverage any incremental U.S. construction and infrastructure spend, which should be supplemented by successful execution of cost reduction of value enhancing projects."

Nucor (NUE), the largest U.S. steel producer as well as the biggest market cap name in the group, represents "quality through-cycle steel exposure," Harris says.

The analyst issues stock price targets for Buy-rated Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF), Commercial Metals (CMC) and Nucor (NUE) at $16, $75 and $190, respectively; he also rates Steel Dynamics (STLD), Reliance (RS) and Radius Recycling (RDUS) as Neutral with respective $155, $341 and $22 PTs.

data.title: Small cap momentum stocks as its three-year rate of change is turning into positive around
data.description:
Oppenheimer analysts feel bullish about the Russell 2000 (IWM), adding that the three-year rate of change for it is “curling into positive territory” after one of its worst performing periods on record.

Since 1928, December has been the best-performing month of the year for momentum stocks, as investors often sell losing stocks to offset capital gains tax liability.

The momentum factor tracks the performance of leaders vs. laggards over a prior-12-month basis. Subsequently, January is the worst-performing month for the momentum factor “because the prior year’s ‘losers’ are bought back — this is the January Effect,” analysts wrote.

In addition, there was $44B in inflows to domestic equity mutual funds and ETFs for the week ending Nov. 15, according to the Investment Company Institute (or ICI).

This is the largest weekly inflow since March 2021. However, Oppenheimer analysts see this as a contrarian concern though the March 2021 spike that happened nine months before the S&P 500’s (SP500) January 2022 peak.

“We believe investor complacency [in large cap] is a yellow light balanced by market positives elsewhere,” analysts wrote in a technical analysis.

Here are the small-cap momentum stocks analysts highlighted, all buy-rated, with their Seeking Alpha quant rating:

  • Shake Shack Inc. (SHAK) – Quant rating: 3.41
  • Clearway Energy Inc. (CWEN) – Quant rating: 2.93
  • The Lovesac Co. (LOVE) – Quant rating: 3.80
  • Itron Inc. (ITRI) – Quant rating: 4.68
  • Consensus Cloud Solutions Inc. (CCSI) – Quant rating: 3.14
  • Genius Sports Ltd. (GENI) – Quant rating: 3.46
  • Compass Inc. (COMP) – Quant rating: 3.39
  • Turtle Beach Corp. (HEAR) – Quant rating: 2.99

data.title: JPMorgan Chase fined $1.8M for overcharging for bond trades by Singapore's central bank
First Solar seen benefiting as U.S. imposes steep anti-dumping rates on panel imports
data.description:
First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) +3.1% in Monday's trading following Friday's release by the U.S. Commerce Department of its long-awaited preliminary determination for anti-dumping duties from southeast Asian countries comprising more than 70% of U.S. solar panel imports.

The Commerce Department calculated anti-dumping rates of 271.28% for imports from Vietnam, 125.37% for Cambodia, 77.85% for Thailand and 21.31% for Malaysia, while major manufacturers have their own company-specific rates.

First Solar (FSLR) is the sole beneficiary from the tariffs, Piper Sandler analyst Kashy Harrison says in reiterating her Overweight rating while raising her price target to $250 from $210, while Nextracker (NXT), Array Technologies (ARRY) and Shoals Technologies (SHLS) are viewed as negatively impacted.

The duties could benefit First Solar's (FSLR) bookings in 2025 "as the Inflation Reduction Act's fate under Republican control becomes clearer thereby extending the higher-for-longer ASP window," Harrison writes, adding that if project delays materialize in 2025, the company's odds of shipping Indian volumes to the U.S. increases.

More generally, the tariff hike was largely in line with expectations, Citi analyst Pierre Lau says, adding that in the longer term, the duties would encourage more production in the U.S., thus replacing imports.

Also, Enphase Energy (ENPH) +3.8% in Monday's trading.

data.title: Gilead Sciences ticks upward for the eight consecutive session
data.description:
Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) shares continued gains for the eighth straight session, as the stock was up 1.5% on Monday.

The biopharmaceutical firm added 4.5% in the preceding seven sessions. The stock has surged over 15% so far this year, compared to an over 26% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

GILD is up 6% over the past one month.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, GILD has a Hold rating with a score of 3.5 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, A- in momentum, while it got a D+ growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 19 analysts gave GILD a Buy or higher rating, while 13 gave it a Hold rating. No analysts recommended the stock as Sell.

Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Most recently, Gilead Sciences acquired assets related to a therapeutic HIV vaccine under development from Barcelona, Spain-based biotech Aelix Therapeutics.

“Gilead Sciences' share price has surged, driven by strong HIV portfolio performance. However, the company appears overvalued based on earnings,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

The firm's Q3 financial results beat on both lines, and it raised its 2024 sales and non-GAAP EPS.

data.title: Corbus, Viking initiated at overweight by Piper Sandler
data.description:
Piper Sandler has initiated coverage of Corbus Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:CRBP) and Viking Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VKTX) with overweight ratings.

The investment bank said it believes Corbus’s (CRBP) drug candidate CRB-701 is differentiated from rivals and could allow for an improved safety profile while encouraging efficacy in the treatment of cervical cancer and other solid tumors. It sees peak risk-adjusted sales of $555M for the product.

Piper also highlighted Corbus’s (CRBP) drug candidate CRB-913, which it believes could take a share of the obesity market, specifically targeting the maintenance setting and patients who discontinue GLP-1 drugs due to tolerability issues. The bank also sees the drug potentially being combined with incretin therapies.

The bank noted that Phase 1 testing for CRB-913 was expected to begin in Q1, with data in the second half of 2025. Piper set a price target for Corbus (CRBP) of $35.

For Viking (VKTX), Piper cited the company's “broad metabolism pipeline spanning across obesity and MASH with the potential optionality of both subcutaneous and oral VK2735 that would compete in the blockbuster incretin class.”

Piper said it believes oral VK2735 could be the “best” oral incretin in development due to lower observed rates of negative gastrointestinal side effects and competitive efficacy. The bank also highlighted Viking's (VKTX) drug candidate VK2809 for MASH.

Piper set its price target for Viking (VKTX) at $74.
